prep.summary: The last TD Sync—focused heavily on AWS integration paths for the MSR desktop—identified the embedded UI widget as the preferred Proof of Concept path despite organizational friction regarding the eventual decommissioning of Conversant. You must validate the SFO procurement intake process—specifically aligning on early PMO and SFO touchpoints before contracts are finalized—to ensure proper governance. Finally, you need to catch up with Michael Somers—who has no recent mention in the work notes—to finalize the integration strategy and validate these financial pathways.
tasks: Validate the SFO intake process—specifically the early touchpoints for technology procurement and prioritization—before any contracts are signed.
Schedule a catch-up with Michael Somers to align on the SFO validation path and overall integration architecture.
Clarify the AWS integration slide messaging—specifically the 'Path to Production' note—to resolve leadership misinterpretations regarding Conversant decommissioning.
Review the ServiceNow pricing structure—specifically the token-consumption model and the five-month baseline proposal—prior to the upcoming meeting with Dan.
prep.title: TD Sync

prep.summary: You reviewed the May 26 D&S Partners meeting—focusing on the launch of the pre-arrival summary pilot, Bedrock knowledge base risks, and Scan AI RFP planning. Regarding the Agent Assist tech approach, a major architectural debate exists between three UI options—an out-of-the-box AWS workspace, a custom homegrown solution, and the preferred embedded UI widget. Two-way SMS security and Agent Assist setups are driving intense organizational storming—specifically during recent Rapid Architecture Governance sessions meant to align business requirements with strict tech leadership boundaries.
tasks: Resolve the architectural friction between the CTO organization and business teams regarding the preferred embedded UI option for Agent Assist.
Confirm the security protocols and compliance requirements for the Two-Way Texting initiative coming out of the recent Rapid Architecture Governance sessions.
Track the progress of the express RFP for Scan AI and the associated business case analysis for P&C process optimization.
Align with Ramesh and Charlotte on the outcomes of the June 3 joint tech-business readout session.
prep.title: D&S Tech Partners Connect

events.summary: A visions of the future of Practicing Architecture

prep.summary: Your work notes lack direct references to "Architecture as code"—focusing instead on modular API integrations, workflow automation, and rapid governance. You must drive the transition away from legacy, manual systems toward programmatically defined pipelines—specifically leveraging AWS API integrations and automated Fivetran data flows. Standardizing these architectural patterns remains critical—requiring clear RACI frameworks for your Integration Architects to ensure process compliance across all upcoming migrations.
tasks: Establish a clear RACI matrix—outlining specific technical decision-making responsibilities—to assist Integration Architects with system transitions.
Assess the API readiness of existing platforms to facilitate agentic workflows and programmatic system integrations.
Implement strict optimization guidelines—targeting token consumption and licensing—to control ServiceNow and generative AI tool costs.
Decommission legacy, manual processes by replacing them with programmatically defined UI components and automated pipelines.
prep.title: A visions of the future of Practicing Architecture

events.summary: Architecture Overview Session with Ryan Beauchamp

prep.summary: You established a strategic technology partnership during your introductory meeting with Ryan Beauchamp—aligning the National Programs team under the retail storefront with the CTO architecture organization. The discussion focused on digital transformation efforts within P&C insurance—specifically navigating transitions from Khoros to Sprinkler, two-way texting, and new guidewire platforms. You committed to follow-up deep dives regarding billing, chat, and social—requiring coordination with key architects and directors.
tasks: Schedule the follow-up deep-dive meeting with Ryan Beauchamp to review billing, chat, and social business areas.
Coordinate invitees for the upcoming sessions—specifically ensuring Brett Kilboss, Natasha Jeffords, Brian Klusman, and James Carris are included.
Request the promised architectural overview documentation detailing the team structure and responsibilities.
Align the National Programs roadmap with ongoing DNS transformation initiatives—focusing on the transition from Khoros to Sprinkler.
prep.title: Architecture Overview Session with Ryan Beauchamp

prep.summary: Address the escalating friction between business units pushing for out-of-the-box vendor UIs and technology leaders demanding custom internal builds—a systemic clash stalling both the AWS MSR desktop and Agent Assist initiatives. Resolve the operational capacity deficits immediately—Integration Architects lack the necessary skills for their expanded decision-making roles, key backfills remain frozen, and the transition of pilot applications into production lacks clear governance. Standardize token consumption policies across AI platforms—specifically Microsoft Copilot, Scan AI, and ServiceNow—to prevent unsustainable licensing costs.
tasks: Resolve the architectural deadlock regarding out-of-the-box versus embedded UI options for the Agent Assist and MSR desktop pilots.
Create a RACI matrix and technical support framework to address the skill and capacity gaps of the Integration Architects.
Pause the ServiceNow RFP and establish a baseline for actual token consumption before committing to long-term licensing budgets.
Define clear ownership and STLC requirements for transitioning pilot applications through the NART process into production.
prep.title: Tech BOD
